wiki/reviews/2026-W27.title: Weekly Review: 2026-W27
wiki/reviews/2026-W27.text:
---
title: "Weekly Review: 2026-W27"
type: review
week: 2026-W27
generated: 2026-07-05
---

# Weekly Review: 2026-W27

First week of the brain. Setup, seed, first real ingest, first lint. Small surface, but enough
signal to already point at what to do next.

## What changed this week

- **Brain infrastructure stood up.** Three-layer arch (raw / wiki / .brain), git-tracked,
  four-job automation designed (miner, compile, lint, synth). Session miner queue live; three
  other scripts still on the follow-up list. See journal [[2026-07-05]].
- **Seed source filed:** [[inspire-marketing-founder-brief]]. Gave us entities
  [[inspire-marketing]], [[meta-ads]], [[local-service-businesses]] and the core discipline
  [[lead-generation]].
- **First real source ingested:** [[burn-money-meta-ads]] (Zack Paid X thread, eight-step
  operator-failure playbook). This one source generated seven concepts:
  [[cost-per-lead]], [[close-rate]], [[campaign-learning-phase]], [[success-metrics-for-ads]],
  [[operator-discipline]], [[targeting-strategy]], [[budget-scaling-rule]].
- **Lint pass strengthened the graph.** [[lead-generation]] now cross-links to five downstream
  concepts; [[inspire-marketing]] now links to [[operator-discipline]]. No orphans, no broken
  links. All stubs ([[offer-stack]], [[ad-creative-patterns]]) intentional.
- **Counts as of end-of-week:** 2 sources, 3 entities, 8 concepts, 1 overview, 1 journal, 1 review.

## What is drifting or contradictory

- **Single-source concentration.** Every non-founder concept in the brain comes from ONE X
  thread. Seven concepts, zero triangulation. The wiki is currently repeating one operator's
  framing back to itself under different headings. That is confirmation bias waiting to happen.
- **Unverified author credibility.** [[burn-money-meta-ads]] cites "$10M+ generated for
  service-based businesses" from the author (UNVERIFIED, correctly tagged). We are absorbing his
  numbers ("50 conversions/week", "20-40% higher CPL during learning", "disciplined operators
  pay 20-30% less per lead") as if they were platform truth. They are one operator's claim.
  These should be labeled more visibly as source-attributed, not treated as laws.
- **Wiki asserts more than sources support (thin spots):**
  - [[campaign-learning-phase]] says CPL is "typically 20-40% higher" during learning. The
    source [[burn-money-meta-ads]] does not state a percentage range. This is invention.
    Flag and either cite or soften.
  - [[operator-discipline]] says disciplined operators "pay 20-30% less per lead". Same issue:
    the source says they pay "significantly less," not a range. Flag and soften.
  - [[targeting-strategy]] says "Meta knows 52,000+ data points on every user." Not present in
    [[burn-money-meta-ads]]. Unsourced. Either cite or remove.
- **Founder brief promised a lever we haven't built.** The brief names "fast lead follow-up"
  as decisive. Not a concept page yet. Speed-to-lead is arguably the highest-ROI concept for
  a local-service lead-gen agency, and it's missing entirely.
- **Zero Inspire-native evidence.** Clients (LiftN'Go, National Car Store, US Home Builders)
  are named as an entity fact but zero CPL, close rate, show rate, or ROAS data has flowed
  from ~/Clients/<client>/CONTEXT.md into the brain. The brain is currently 100% theoretical
  playbook, 0% Inspire's own book of business.
- **Client entity pages missing.** The three named clients exist as strings inside
  [[inspire-marketing]] but have no entity pages. If we intend to synthesize across clients,
  they need pages.
- **No contradiction resolution muscle yet.** [[cost-per-lead]] and [[success-metrics-for-ads]]
  both note that industry education "teaches the wrong thing" but we haven't ingested the
  opposing view. When we do, there is no page primed to hold the argument.

## What deserves attention

Three highest-leverage threads for next week, in order:

1. **Triangulate the Zack Paid claims with a second source.** Highest priority. Ingest one
   independent piece on Meta learning phase and one on scaling / broad targeting (Motion's
   creative studies, Foreplay teardowns, Meta's own docs on the 50-conversion rule). If the
   second source disagrees, the wiki gets much smarter fast. If it agrees, the claims graduate
   from "one operator says" to "converged pattern."
2. **Build the missing speed-to-lead concept.** The founder brief already flags it as decisive
   for local service. Even a stub with two or three sourced points would prevent us from
   under-weighting a lever that beats most creative work. This is also the concept most likely
   to show up in Inspire's client CONTEXT.md files.
3. **Pull one real number from the Inspire book.** Even one line ("NCS is running at X CPL
   with Y show rate on Meta in June 2026") converts the brain from "book about lead-gen" to
   "compounding record of Inspire's own edge." That is the entire point.

Next sources worth finding: Meta's official learning-phase documentation; one detailed creative
teardown from a serious agency (Motion, Foreplay, Common Thread); one landing-page conversion
benchmark study for local service. Also: whatever Yaniv already has in
`~/Clients/<client>/CONTEXT.md` that could be filed as a source.

Sharpest open questions:

- What is the CPL / close-rate baseline across Inspire's current three clients? Without this,
  every "maximum allowable CPL" concept in the wiki is abstract.
- Does the 50-conversion/week rule survive intact for CBO campaigns and Advantage+ Shopping?
  The Zack Paid thread doesn't distinguish, and Advantage+ changes the rules.
- Are broad-targeting-plus-creative-diversity playbooks robust across niches, or do
  low-consideration niches (used-car dealer, vehicle transport) need different tactics than
  high-consideration niches (construction / remodel)?

## Thesis check

The current thesis in [[overview]] reads:

> The moat is operational reliability plus proof: consistent lead quality and honest re

os/skills-registry.title: Skills Registry
os/skills-registry.text:
---
title: Skills Registry
type: os
updated: 2026-07-08
---

# Skills Registry

Every installed skill and what to say to trigger it. Generated from `~/.claude/skills/` on 2026-07-08 (29 skills + 5 commands + 5 vault Obsidian skills = 39).

## Client production lines

- **inventory-drop** · "inventory drop" / "/inventory-drop": NCS 6-vehicle ad layouts, 3 gates
- **ushb-video** · "next video": USHB UGC picture chains + scripts (program in maintenance)
- **testimonial-overlay** · "testimonial overlay": Remotion alpha overlay packs from a transcript
- **client-intake-form** · "intake form for [client]": branded fillable HTML questionnaire, emailed submissions
- **client-monthly-report** · "monthly report": polished client performance PDF
- **meta-launch-kit** · "launch the campaign": Ads Manager manual-launch checklist and traps

## Content and research

- **deep-research** · "deep research [topic]": multi-source cited report
- **market-pack** · "/market-pack [niche]": full market domination knowledge pack
- **weekly-content-engine** · Inspire content pillars and batch rules
- **humanizer** · "humanize this": strip AI tells from writing
- **brand** · brand voice and messaging frameworks

## Generation (Higgsfield)

- **higgsfield-run** · THE job runner: submit, wait, download, verify, cost line
- **higgsfield-generate** · images and video (GPT Image 2, Seedance, Nano Banana)
- **higgsfield-product-photoshoot** · brand-quality product shots
- **higgsfield-marketplace-cards** · marketplace listing image sets
- **higgsfield-soul-id** · train a face-faithful Soul character

## Design

- **design** / **banner-design** / **slides** / **design-system** / **ui-styling** / **ui-ux-pro-max** / **power-design** · logos, banners, decks, tokens, UI (ui-ux-pro-max styled the IRIS dashboard)
- **open-design** · ground work in the local Open Design repo (149 design systems)
- **redesign-skill** · "redesign this site/app": audits an existing design and upgrades it to premium quality without breaking it
- **taste-skill** · anti-slop frontend for landing pages and portfolios; ships interfaces that do not look templated

## Ops and QA

- **creative-qa** · "QA the images": pre-ship sweep of AI creatives (text, dashes, logos)
- **ops-console** · "build a console": single-file HTML operating console from any run sheet
- **setup-audit** · "audit my setup": mine past sessions for automation opportunities

## Commands

- **/carousel** · styled HTML carousel in the Inspire brand system
- **/day-brief** · pull today's calendar + inbox scan + news into the IRIS MY DAY briefing
- **/deploy-html** · deploy any HTML to a live surge URL
- **/pipeline-status** · outreach + content pipeline digest
- **/package-skill** · zip a skill for claude.ai upload

## Vault-local (Obsidian, official kepano set)

Installed in `.claude/skills/` of this vault; active when Claude runs here:
- **obsidian-markdown** · correct Obsidian-flavored markdown editing
- **obsidian-bases** · create and edit .base database views
- **json-canvas** · create and edit .canvas boards
- **obsidian-cli** · drive the Obsidian app from the command line
- **defuddle** · clean article extraction from web pages

## Rule

If a skill you name is not in this list, it does not exist; Claude should say so and point to the closest real one.

# IRIS Directives

Edit this note in Obsidian. The top 3 unchecked items appear on the IRIS dashboard as today's directives. Check a box to mark it done (it shows crossed out). The heartbeat picks up changes within 3 hours, or immediately when run manually.

- [ ] 🌴 5 live conversations this week · log them in os/conversations
- [ ] CLOSE #1 by Aug 15 · get Ron's FIRST intro + message the 14 old repliers · MB Air shelved to fall
- [ ] Film the 1:59 DM pitch video · script with timing marks in ~/inspire-pipeline/dm_sorcery_copy.md section 2 · it unlocks the whole DM pipeline

Parked below the fold (edit or promote as needed):

- [ ] Every reply answered within 2 hours · Florida lands Feb 1, 2027
- [ ] MB Air nurture: check-in message ~Sep 15 (owner says no capacity; fall = slow season)
- [ ] Ship the NCS inventory drop batch (gate 2 approved, fill wells + QA)
- [ ] USHB campaign health check + weekly maintenance pass
- [x] Refill construction v2 funnel when Apify credit resets (done: pool at 3,660, daily scrape live)

wiki/reviews/2026-W28.title: Weekly Review: 2026-W28
wiki/reviews/2026-W28.text:
---
title: "Weekly Review: 2026-W28"
type: review
week: 2026-W28
generated: 2026-07-12
---

# Weekly Review: 2026-W28

Second full week. If W27 was setup and seed, W28 is the week the brain met the business. Real
outreach machinery, first hot HVAC lead run end to end, five new concept pages, a fourth hard
rule added to the founder's operating contract (Rules 13-16, proven before clever), and the
Claude tooling layer stitched in. The wiki roughly doubled in surface area. It is now big enough
to have its own drift, and it does.

## What changed this week

- **Counts moved.** From 2 sources, 3 entities, 8 concepts at end of W27 to 5 sources, 10
  entities, 19 concepts at end of W28. Roughly 2.4x on entities, 2.4x on concepts, 2.5x on
  sources. Journal volume jumped from a handful of KB to ~160KB across seven days, most of it
  on 2026-07-09 alone.
- **Two new source pages triangulate the theory shelf.**
  [[meta-learning-phase-docs]] confirms the 50-events / 7-day rule and edit-restart behavior
  first heard from Zack Paid. [[prompting-fable-5]] and [[fable-5-memory-system]] bring in the
  agent-prompting and memory-cycle concepts underneath the automation layer.
- **Real outreach machine went from design to running code.** [[cold-email-system]] and
  [[instagram-dm-outreach]] were promoted from stubs to full concept pages, backed by a
  ~7,000-word / 143-source deep-research pass and an 8-phase execution console. Stage A is
  live sending on the main domain (yaniv@inspireaigroup.com, 25/day, 15-30 min spacing, plain
  text, DNS auth clean). First cold email sent 2026-07-09. Snapshot as of 2026-07-09: 2,731
  leads, 2,609 contacted, 8 replies, ~0.31% reply rate (see caveat below).
- **Offer, agreement, and homepage got structural.** [[offer-structure]] now holds the whole
  pricing and guarantee stack: $1,250 launch / $2,500 ongoing, four guarantee locks (min
  qualified-lead count, ad-budget floor $1,000-$1,500/mo, exact qualified-lead definition,
  2-hour speed-to-lead condition), one revision round per deliverable, AI presenter disclosure,
  client owns the ad account. inspireaigroup.com is live on Netlify with an AI-trust FAQ, a
  proof bar (363 leads, $7.37 CPL, $2,676 revenue), and an exclusivity callout.
- **First hot HVAC prospect run end to end.** [[mb-air-hvac]] (Margate FL, Brazilian-owned)
  got the first full closing kit: bilingual demo landing page (mbair-demo.vercel.app), open-angle
  recon, a company deep-dive, English + Portuguese proposal PDFs, and a live 2026-07-09 call.
  Not yet signed. Every rule that ended up in Rules 13-16 came out of this deal.
- **CLAUDE.md gained Rules 13-16 (proven before clever).** Codified into the founder contract:
  copy what an ad market has already proven works, treat saturation as a buy signal, never let
  UNPROVEN or HYPOTHESIS labels quietly upgrade in later chat, never let market facts (a
  demographic, a regulation) act as proof of an untested angle. See [[proven-play]] for the
  framework and its red-team.
- **Automation layer closed its recall gap.** The SessionStart hook now injects a
  read-only pointer to this brain's `index.md` into every Claude session (except the brain's
  own `CLAUDE_BRAIN=1` runs). Client-work sessions (NCS, USHB, MB Air, cold outreach) can
  finally pull the brain's lessons on demand. See [[session-memory-cycle]].
- **New client and prospect entities.** [[national-car-store]] (NCS: FL used commercial
  vehicle dealer, Meta special-ad-category split, bad-credit hook), [[us-home-builders]] (LA
  remodel: 33-video AI UGC program in progress), [[mb-air-hvac]], [[hvac-florida-market]],
  [[roofing-niche]], [[med-spa-niche]]. Client-facing surface area jumped from a single line
  in [[inspire-marketing]] to five entities and one niche-level page.
- **Meta platform signal captured.** Advantage+ Leads rolled out globally with a reported ~14%
  lower CPL, plus SMS and work-email lead verification and a 24/7 nurture agent. All
  UNVERIFIED, all filed against [[meta-ads]] and [[ad-creative-patterns]].

## What is drifting or contradictory

- **The single-source problem got wider, not narrower.** W27 flagged that every non-founder
  concept came from one Zack Paid thread. Meta docs partially closed that (learning-phase
  claims are now triangulated), but four of this week's biggest concepts ([[cold-email-system]],
  [[instagram-dm-outreach]], [[offer-structure]], [[content-strategy]]) are backed almost
  entirely by "journal 2026-07-09" plus one internal deep-research report. That is the brain
  citing its own workflow, not the market. Bulk of the cited numbers (0.31% reply rate,
  $9-14 per 1,000 leads via Apify, $37/10k on MillionVerifier, IG DM 25/day safety cap,
  60-120 second delay, roofing $150-300/lead tolerance) are marked UNVERIFIED but sit inside
  concept pages that read as playbook. Attribution shows the caveat; skimming does not.
- **Homepage proof stats have an internal contradiction.** [[inspire-marketing]] flags it:
  the $2,676 figure is labeled "revenue" on the site and "ad spend" in the session journal.
  A second anchor ($29K revenue) has no confirmed source. These sit on a public homepage. This
  is exactly the kind of unverified proof Rule 3 (never fabricate) exists to prevent, and it
  has been open since 2026-07-08 without reconciliation. Highest-cost open thread on the
  wiki right now, because it is public-facing.
- **Advantage+ Leads "14% lower CPL" is spreading.** It came in through the 2026-07-11
  day-brief news pull, tagged UNVERIFIED, but it already touches [[meta-ads]],
  [[lead-generation]], and [[ad-creative-patterns]]. If it turns out to be a Meta marketing
  number rather than an independent measurement, three pages have to walk back at once.
  Verify before it hardens into thesis.
- **MB Air landing page has a $8,000-$13,000 replacement figure it may not own.** Labeled
  HYPOTHESIS in [[mb-air-hvac]]. If it drove clicks or was quoted to Anna or Luiz, and it
  turns out to be resea

index.title: Index: Second Brain Catalog
index.text:
# Index: Second Brain Catalog

The catalog of every page in the wiki. Read this first when answering a question, then drill
into the pages it points to. Updated on every ingest.

**Counts:** 10 sources · 12 entities · 34 concepts · 1 overview · 3 reviews

---

## Overview
- [[overview]]: the big-picture synthesis and evolving thesis for this marketing brain

## Reviews
- [[2026-W27]]: first week of the brain: setup, seed, Zack Paid ingest, single-source risk, thesis sharpened
- [[2026-W28]]: brain met the business: outreach system live, first HVAC prospect end-to-end, Rules 13-16 (proven-first) added, thesis expanded to proven-first + show-the-work
- [[2026-W29]]: business got a clock (Florida ladder) and a second lever: CRO shelf ingested, first real Ad Library evidence, whale/referral offer, MB Air live; thesis sharpened to conversion on both sides of the funnel

## Entities
- [[inspire-marketing]]: Yaniv's agency: Meta lead-gen + AI UGC video for local service businesses (Florida); inspireaigroup.com live on Netlify; $2k-2.5k retainer offer
- [[meta-ads]]: the primary paid channel (Facebook / Instagram lead-gen); updated Jul 2026 with Advantage+ Leads and organic reach signals
- [[local-service-businesses]]: the core market Inspire serves; identity beats lead-count framing, trust beats price (Houzz: 48% "finding someone I trust" is the #1 struggle)
- [[roofing-niche]]: target niche; avg job ~$9,528 UNVERIFIED; owners pay $150-300/lead; strongest ROI pitch
- [[med-spa-niche]]: target niche for IG DM lane; patient LTV $9k-30k UNVERIFIED; best AI UGC fit
- [[us-home-builders]]: LA remodel client (USHB), legal entity Flawless Construction Inc.; 66 static ads done (14 winners); new branded landing page live at ushb-estimate.com; license is B General Building only; Ron now also a referral partner; real lead data shows the answer-rate leak is contact rate, not closing
- [[national-car-store]]: West Palm Beach FL used commercial vehicle dealer (NCS); real rating 4.7/775 (old ad badges say 4.8/700+); two-tier Meta compliance split now extends to landing pages and lead-qualifier fields; inventory page at nationalcarstore-wpb.surge.sh, financing page moving to floridacarstore.com (Vercel, SSL) with DNS still pending
- [[mb-air-hvac]]: Margate FL HVAC prospect; SHELVED 2026-07-19 (no capacity in peak season); proven-play pull rebuilt the offer around the $69 tune-up + same-day repair, Portuguese-first demoted to a capped test; Jobber wired live; nurture check-in ~Sep 15
- [[hvac-florida-market]]: HVAC Florida market niche; formal proven-play pull confirms $59-90 tune-up + same-day repair as PROVEN; EPA AIM Act (R-410A banned Jan 2025); distinct from USHB's SoCal HVAC niche; peak-season capacity objections are a timing problem, pitch the slow-season pipeline not more jobs
- [[claude-fable-5]]: Anthropic's most capable model; specialist for long autonomous work; pricier than Opus 4.8; not a general replacement
- [[zack-paid]]: @zackpaid, operator running Inspire's exact done-for-you Meta ads model; author of 2 brain sources; tactical claims triangulate, self-reported revenue UNVERIFIED
- [[clay]]: data enrichment/prospecting platform tested for cold outreach sourcing; LinkedIn search wrong fit for small shops, local-business waterfall path UNVERIFIED

## Concepts
- [[ai-tool-quality]]: evaluating which AI platform (Claude vs. Grok, etc.) fits specific tasks
- [[lead-generation]]: turning ad spend into qualified inbound leads for a business
- [[cost-per-lead]]: backwards-calculated from business economics (ACV * close rate = max CPL)
- [[close-rate]]: percentage of booked calls that close, foundation for lead economics
- [[campaign-learning-phase]]: the 5-7 day stabilization period when Meta algorithm exits exploration mode
- [[success-metrics-for-ads]]: hierarchy of metrics from CPAC down to CPC (only top 6 matter)
- [[operator-discipline]]: systematic rules (72-hour freeze, once-daily check, day-5-7 evaluation) that reduce CPL
- [[targeting-strategy]]: broad targeting + strong creative outperforms narrow targeting + weak creative
- [[budget-scaling-rule]]: scale in 20% increments every 2-3 days to avoid resetting algorithm
- [[video-creative-strategy]]: dual-purpose 1:1 motion loops for web + Instagram; now includes Higgsfield/Seedance AI video production techniques
- [[ugc-video-strategy]]: multi-presenter AI UGC ad system; varied cast, native Seedance speech, FEEL-THINK-ACT, three proof formats
- [[content-strategy]]: two-layer taxonomy (Trust Arsenal bank + weekly engine); batch generation; backlog gate
- [[cold-email-system]]: Stage A free-tier cold outreach (25-40/day, main domain); Stage B gates on first paid client; Gift Drop close; bounce rate holding ~10% across three readings, still an open risk; an old replier list needs a real thread-by-thread re-read, not a headcount
- [[instagram-dm-outreach]]: automated Instagram DM cold outreach for high-visual-adoption niches; 25/day cap, text-only opener; primary lane for med spas; unresolved tension over volume vs. precision-layer framing
- [[ad-creative-patterns]]: FEEL-THINK-ACT script, PAS vs. Before-After-Bridge by purchase type, Advantage+ Leads, creative fatigue 14-21 days, 2026 platform signals (all UNVERIFIED), USHB 14-winners-of-66 patterns
- [[offer-structure]]: Inspire pilot agreement mechanics: $1,250/$2,500 pricing, 4 guarantee locks, Gift Drop close, foot-in-the-door offer pattern, three-question guarantee-clock check from a real sales-call save
- [[proven-play]]: framework for recommending only what ad markets have proven works; saturation = proof; Proof Density formula; PROVEN/VALIDATED-LITE/UNPROVEN tiers; a true mechanism fact is not automatically the right hook
- [[agent-prompting-patterns]]: five instructions that keep an unattended AI agent honest and bounded (progress audit, proactivity limit, memory, delegation, clean final answer)
- [[effort-calibration]]: AI effort setting is a cost/latency trade-off, not a quality knob;

# Life

Personal layer of the OS. Mostly TO FILL: tell Claude facts to pin here (or edit directly in Obsidian) and the OS will use them for planning, briefings, and directives.

## Known

- Based in Israel (Holon area), works US-client hours across time zones
- Goes to the gym (fire-and-forget mode exists for gym/driving windows)

## TO FILL

- Health: training schedule, goals
- Family: birthdays, important dates
- Personal projects and learning goals
- Travel plans
- Ideal week: when deep work happens, when calls happen

# US Home Builders (USHB) · MAINTENANCE MODE

**What.** LA construction and remodeling company. The UGC production push is over; the job now is managing accounts and keeping campaigns healthy.

Full canonical facts: `~/Clients/ushb/CONTEXT.md` (this note is status only).

## Maintenance scope

- Manage the Meta accounts, keep campaigns healthy
- Weekly health check (see [[IRIS Directives]])
- Ads Manager is driven MANUALLY: the USHB ad account is not Meta-MCP-enabled; use the meta-launch-kit skill checklist

## If production restarts

- `/ushb-video` skill: UGC picture chains + scripts only (Yaniv renders videos himself in the Higgsfield UI, Claude never generates USHB videos)
- Brand accent: burnt orange #b3541e

wiki/reviews/2026-W29.title: Weekly Review: 2026-W29
wiki/reviews/2026-W29.text:
---
title: "Weekly Review: 2026-W29"
type: review
week: 2026-W29
generated: 2026-07-19
---

# Weekly Review: 2026-W29

Third full week. W27 was setup and seed. W28 was the week the brain met the business. W29 is the
week the business got a clock and a second lever. Two things happened at once: the theory shelf
grew a whole new pillar (conversion / CRO, from two real outside sources), and the whole operation
got pointed at a single dated goal (Florida, Feb 1 2027, $10k per month by Nov 15, closes #1 to #4
on a fixed ladder). The wiki roughly doubled its concept count again, for the third week running.
It is now less an abstract marketing library and more the strategy layer under a specific revenue
sprint. That is a good problem, and it changes what "drift" means: the risk is no longer thin
sourcing alone, it is thin sourcing on numbers that are now backing live ad spend and a public
homepage.

## What changed this week

- **Counts moved again.** From W28 end (5 sources, 10 entities, 19 concepts) to now (9 sources,
  12 entities, 30 concepts). Plus 4 sources, plus 2 entities, plus 11 concepts. Third straight
  week of roughly 2x concept growth. The 07-19 lint added the last 3 stub pages
  ([[ai-tool-quality]], [[inspire-pipeline-automation]], [[whale-package-offer]]).
- **A second theory pillar arrived: conversion / CRO.** Two real outside sources ingested 07-16:
  [[hormozi-landing-page-strategy]] (ThrillX analysis of Hormozi, 16 landing-page frameworks) and
  [[morgan-cold-dm-system]] (Charlie Morgan DM Sorcery permission ladder). These seeded
  [[value-equation]], [[landing-page-cro]], [[cro-testing-cadence]], and [[permission-based-dm]].
  Up to now the brain's theory was almost all traffic-side (turn spend into leads). This week it
  grew a conversion-side shelf: fix the funnel before buying more traffic, put 80-90% of effort
  above the fold, express all four Value Equation levers, pick a testing method by traffic volume.
  This is the framework backing that was missing under "Make Everything, Fix All the Funnels."
- **First real in-market evidence entered the brain.** [[ushb-proven-plays]] (ingested 07-16) is
  the first source that is not a founder brief, an operator thread, or an internal deep-research
  pass. It is a dated Meta Ad Library pull (2026-07-14, expires 2026-07-28): "Fast Free Quote /
  24-hour response" is the deepest-proven SoCal home-improvement offer (15+ distinct active
  advertisers, oldest still live 22.4 weeks). This is [[proven-play]] going from framework to
  field use, and it produced the framework's first NULL PLAY results too (Foundations, Tankless,
  Windows returned too-thin evidence to certify).
- **A new revenue structure: the whale package plus a referral partner.** USHB's Ron became a
  referral partner, not just a colleague. New offer tier: $2,000 per month base plus $125 per
  shown appointment plus $75 per closed job, $5,000 per month minimum ad spend, tiered creative.
  Split with Ron: base 50/50, performance bonuses 70/30 toward Yaniv (whoever does fulfillment).
  Two client-ready PDFs built on the zero-credit HTML-to-Chrome pipeline. New pages
  [[whale-package-offer]] and [[referral-partner-split-structure]]. This is the first
  client-acquisition path Inspire has that is not cold outreach.
- **The business got a spine and a clock: Florida 2027.** Move date locked to Feb 1 2027. Close
  ladder: Close #1 (Luiz / [[mb-air-hvac]]) by Aug 15, then Sep 15, Oct 15, Nov 15 (reaching
  $10k per month). The primary tracked metric flipped from machine sends to real conversations
  (5 per week goal). IRIS dashboard and the "Florida Wall" day-grid were built around this. The
  math is on file at [[florida-2027-math]] in memory; the point for this brain is that its
  purpose narrowed from "win in local lead-gen" to "close 4 to 5 clients in the next four months."
- **MB Air went live and is selling.** The demo landing page (mbair-demo.vercel.app) was wired to
  MB Air's real Jobber booking link (Web3Forms removed per the prospect's ask) and is now backing
  real ad spend. Two soft claims baked in on Yaniv's explicit informed call: "12+ years" and
  "Licensed and Insured" (see drift below).
- **A durable creative rule crystallized.** Never depict traditional filming gear (cameras, ring
  lights, tripods, crews) in any Inspire creative, because the entire pitch is that the video ads
  are AI-generated and the client never films anything. Caught by Yaniv when a "content studio"
  hero shot was QA'd as a keeper. Filed against [[ad-creative-patterns]] and [[inspire-marketing]].
- **Two frameworks got named that back the "show the work" pillar.** [[proof-paradox]] (a real
  $500-to-$55K case study got called "nice ad bro" on r/FacebookAds, a true claim rejected on
  sight) and [[lead-platform-abuse]] (shared-lead platforms resell leads 3 to 8 times; FTC fined
  HomeAdvisor up to $7.2M) both sharpen why honest process beats naked claims for this buyer.
- **Infrastructure matured, mostly outside this brain's schema but load-bearing operationally.**
  Cron is confirmed dead on this Mac (the IG reply-checker had been dark since May 4). Secrets
  scrubbed out of config.py. The leads.json split-brain was unified to one canonical file. The
  email engine and IG reply-checker were both hardened. The brain's own compile model was
  upgraded from Haiku to Sonnet after Haiku fabricated numbers on permanent pages.
- **The brain started forking to a second AI tool.** Yaniv bundled 57 vault files into one file
  for a Grok project (07-15, 07-18). The knowledge base is now snapshotted into Grok as a parallel
  surface. See [[ai-tool-quality]].

## What is drifting or contradictory

- **The unaudited-numbers problem got wider this week, not narrower.** Both new outside sources
  are built openly on self-reported claims. Hormozi / ThrillX lift figures (130%, 64%, 50-70%,
  $3M) are, in the source's own words, "creator-reported agency claims, not independently audited
  facts." Mo

os/about-me.title: About Me · Yaniv Yonatanov
os/about-me.text:
---
title: About Me
type: os
updated: 2026-07-07
---

# About Me · Yaniv Yonatanov

**Who.** Founder of Inspire Marketing (INSPIRE MARKETING AI LLC). Meta lead generation for local service businesses, focused on Florida. Based in Israel (operates on Israel time), clients in the US.

**Not a developer.** Explain technical steps simply, click by click for third-party consoles. Dictates by voice, so expect phonetic typos.

## How I work (hard rules for any agent)

- Plan first, show it, wait for my green light before spending anything
- Zero em dashes and en dashes anywhere, including inside generated images
- Never fabricate proof, stats, reviews, or sources; mark unproven claims UNVERIFIED
- Test on one, get approval, then batch; never regenerate approved creatives
- Show every deliverable: open it and print the path
- Cost line after every spend; free-tier first
- Insurance before destruction: archive before deleting or replacing
- Summaries in 30 seconds, lead with the outcome

## Contact and accounts

- Email: yaniv@inspireaigroup.com
- Secrets live in `~/.secrets/` (one file per credential), never in chat
- Client context packs: `~/Clients/<client>/CONTEXT.md`

## TO FILL (say it and I will add it)

- Goals for 2026 (revenue target, client count, content goals)
- Weekly routine (gym days, deep work blocks, family time)

log.title: Log: Second Brain History
log.text:
# Log: Second Brain History

Append-only, chronological. Every entry starts with `## [YYYY-MM-DD] <ingest|query|lint> | title`
so it is greppable: `grep "^## \[" log.md | tail -5` shows the last five events.

---

## [2026-07-21] mine | NCS landing page build, mobile pass, photo shoot, one-generation-gate incident
Filed a full journal entry for session 7f7fd204 (National Car Store). Two-tier landing page built
and deployed (nationalcarstore-wpb.surge.sh), mobile pass, Higgsfield photo shoot, qualifier
questions added, custom domain (floridacarstore.com) stood up on Vercel. Also logs a real blow-up:
a bad image-to-image generation and an "untouched" claim that was not verified against the source,
which produced two new hard rules (verify-before-claiming-untouched, one-generation-then-approve).

## [2026-07-05] setup | Second brain initialized
Created schema (CLAUDE.md), index.md, log.md, folder structure (raw/, wiki/), and overview.md.
Focus set to marketing & business.

## [2026-07-05] ingest | Inspire Marketing founder brief (seed)
Filed [[inspire-marketing-founder-brief]]. Created entities [[inspire-marketing]], [[meta-ads]],
[[local-service-businesses]] and concept [[lead-generation]]. Updated overview and index.
This is a demo/seed ingest to show the machine working; expand or replace with real sources.

## [2026-07-05] mine | Setup session analyzed
Session 6606fdd5 analyzed. Documented decisions (three-layer arch, four-job automation, queue-based miner),
confirmed patterns (no em-dashes, never-fabricate, test-on-one, git insurance), and follow-ups (remaining
scripts not yet built). No contradictions or marketing learnings (infrastructure session). Journal entry filed.

## [2026-07-13] mine | IRIS voice query cut short, no content
Session d6f54aae: dictation cutoff ("what"), IRIS gave dashboard status readout. No marketing/business content. Journal one-liner filed.

## [2026-07-06] mine | Inspire website redesign + motion asset integration
Session f086aecb analyzed. Full inspireaigroup.com redesign: single-page HTML, white/purple palette, proof bar with real stats, 12 motion assets planned and partially integrated. Journal entry filed with UNVERIFIED flag on proof numbers and open follow-up on deployment and asset-count verification.

## [2026-07-05] compile | Burn Money Meta Ads ingested
Filed [[burn-money-meta-ads]] (Zack Paid X thread: eight-step operator failure playbook). Created 6 new concepts:
[[cost-per-lead]], [[close-rate]], [[campaign-learning-phase]], [[success-metrics-for-ads]], [[operator-discipline]],
[[targeting-strategy]], [[budget-scaling-rule]]. Updated [[meta-ads]] entity with algorithm requirements (50 convs/week),
learning-phase timing (72h), and 20% scaling rule. No contradictions found. Index refreshed: now 2 sources, 7 concepts, 3 entities.

## [2026-07-05] lint | Weekly health check
Scanned all 13 wiki pages. Fixed cross-link gaps: [[lead-generation]] now connects to success-metrics, targeting, operator-discipline,
scaling, and learning-phase. Added [[inspire-marketing]] -> [[operator-discipline]]. All links valid (no orphans, no broken links).
All stubs intentional ([[offer-stack]], [[ad-creative-patterns]]). No contradictions or stale claims. Graph strengthened.

## [2026-07-05] synth | Weekly review 2026-W27 filed, thesis sharpened
Filed [[2026-W27]]. Flagged single-source concentration (all 7 concepts derive from [[burn-money-meta-ads]]) and three unsourced
numbers in the wiki (learning-phase "20-40% higher", operator-discipline "20-30% less", targeting-strategy "52,000+ data points").
Called out missing speed-to-lead concept and zero Inspire-native evidence. Sharpened overview thesis from "operational reliability
plus proof" to "operator discipline plus economics-first math"; preserved prior wording under Thesis history. Next-week priorities:
triangulate Zack Paid with a second source, build speed-to-lead stub, pull one real CPL number from the Inspire book.

## [2026-07-05] ingest | Meta learning-phase official docs (triangulation)
Filed [[meta-learning-phase-docs]] to test [[burn-money-meta-ads]]. Meta CONFIRMS the ~50 optimization
events per 7 days learning rule, that significant edits restart learning, and cost instability below
threshold. Refinement: only "significant edits" reset learning, not every change. Updated
[[campaign-learning-phase]], [[operator-discipline]], [[meta-ads]], overview, index. Sources now 3.

## [2026-07-06] compile | Inspire website learnings promoted from journal
No new raw sources (all 3 already ingested). Promoted 3 durable learnings from 2026-07-06 journal.
Updated [[inspire-marketing]]: added AI UGC video as second service line, two-offer homepage structure,
and public proof stats (363 conv, $7.37 CPL, $2,676 revenue, UNVERIFIED). Created new concept
[[video-creative-strategy]] (dual-purpose 1:1 motion loops for web + Instagram). Index updated: 9 concepts.

## [2026-07-06] mine | LLM Wiki setup: final mine of session 6606fdd5
Session already partially mined on 2026-07-05 (mid-session test). Final mine confirms session complete. Automation verified: hook fired correctly on f086aecb, UNVERIFIED tagging worked automatically. No new marketing learnings; infrastructure only. Remaining automation scripts (nightly-compile, weekly-lint, weekly-synthesis) still pending build.

## [2026-07-07] compile | nothing new
No unfiled raw sources (all 3 already ingested). No new durable marketing learnings in 2026-07-05 or 2026-07-06 journal beyond what prior compile runs already promoted. Wiki is current.

## [2026-07-07] mine | Inspire Content Command Center Phase 1 build
Session 7a10a529. Built mobile HTML cockpit (inspire-content.surge.sh) with two-layer content structure: 26-piece permanent Trust Arsenal Bank tab plus rotating weekly Ads/Story/Quotes tabs. Security fix: replaced leaked master JSONBin key with scoped dashboards-rw key across all dashboards. Master key rotation in JSONBin web dashboard is still open.

## [2026-07-07] m

The front door of the vault. Everything important is one link away.

## Live

- [[us-home-builders]]: USHB (maintenance mode)

## The knowledge brain

- [[index]]: catalog of the marketing wiki (entities, concepts, sources)
- [[overview]]: the evolving big-picture thesis

## How this vault stays alive

Four automations maintain the wiki (session miner, nightly compile, weekly lint, weekly synthesis) and the IRIS heartbeat feeds the dashboard. You never have to file anything by hand.
